attempts.insight: The unfold + exact two-line shape mirrors iter-054's basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen body. Mathlib's Scheme.basicOpen_mul has the forward orientation X.basicOpen (f * g) = X.basicOpen f ⊓ X.basicOpen g; we want the reverse, hence .symm. Named-args (C := C) (U := U) at iSup binder sites are required (both implicit in basicOpenCover def).

The binary form is sufficient for the iter-057+ Čech complex on basic-open covers; n-ary generalisation to Fin (n+1) / Finset indexing is deferred to iter-057+ when the alternating-sum argument structure clarifies the natural shape (the n-ary form has a non-trivial empty-product edge case basicOpen 1 = U ≠ ⊤ that complicates the Finset version).

attempts.insight: Canonical chain-by-rewrite shape: when downstream Mathlib lemma (IsAffineOpen.basicOpen) takes a single basic-open argument but goal carries an intersection, use rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul] to fold intersection back into single basic-open of the product before applying the downstream lemma. Cheaper than a cartesian-product / fiber-product proof.
